title: Hybrid Mixer Refinement
research_question: The lower-four hybrid mixer model is the new frontier, but is that exact placement and mixer shape really optimal?

candidates.name: p1-lower3
candidates.goal: Test whether the current lower-four winner is over-replacing attention.
candidates.hypothesis: Three lower mixers may preserve most of the gain while restoring some lost lower-stack attention expressivity.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the win is broad or narrowly tied to one exact placement.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest first refinement of the current best run.
candidates.description: replace the lower three attention layers with sequence mixers

candidates.name: p2-lower5
candidates.goal: Test whether the frontier wants even less lower-stack attention than the current winner uses.
candidates.hypothesis: The lower stack may tolerate one more mixer layer than the current lower-four design.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the current winner is still too conservative.
candidates.why_worthy: This extends the same architectural idea in the most direct way possible.
candidates.description: replace the lower five attention layers with sequence mixers

candidates.name: p3-lower4-small-mixer
candidates.goal: Test whether the current mixer itself is larger than necessary.
candidates.hypothesis: A smaller mixer may preserve quality while saving bytes and compute inside the winning placement.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the new mechanism is already overbuilt.
candidates.why_worthy: This asks whether the frontier can get both better and cheaper at once.
candidates.description: keep the winning lower-four placement but shrink mixer width

candidates.name: p4-lower4-large-mixer
candidates.goal: Test whether the current mixer width is underpowered.
candidates.hypothesis: A richer mixer may exploit the lower-stack replacement idea better than the current width.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the winning mechanism is capacity-limited.
candidates.why_worthy: If this wins, the frontier is still leaving mixer quality on the table.
candidates.description: keep the winning lower-four placement but widen the mixer

candidates.name: p5-lower4-kernel6
candidates.goal: Test whether the lower-stack mixer wants a wider local receptive field.
candidates.hypothesis: The current kernel may be too narrow, and a slightly broader local window may improve the trade against removed attention.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the mechanism needs more local context rather than more width.
candidates.why_worthy: This probes the quality of the mixer itself, not just where it is placed.
candidates.description: keep the winning lower-four placement but widen the mixer kernel
